description: Simplify savemyhardmoneyloan.com to a minimal single-page lead funnel with light blue theme, distinct from DSCR flagship site.

technical_decisions: Light blue color palette: #2563eb primary, #ffffff background
Single CTA button - no phone numbers anywhere
Pure lead funnel - no direct contact options
Remove NMLS/Cornerstone branding - hard money doesn't require licensure
tradeoffs: Chose minimal design over feature-rich - faster load, clearer conversion path
Removed phone CTAs - forces all leads through quiz for better tracking
edge_cases: Quiz success page simplified - just confirmation message
Footer cross-sell links to DSCR only, not DPA
constraints: DNS managed via Bluehost for savemyhardmoneyloan.com
Keep existing API route for CRM integration

verification_strategy.smoke_test: Load homepage, complete quiz flow, verify form submits successfully and shows 'We'll be in touch soon' message

checkpoints.client: No console errors, quiz progresses through all steps
checkpoints.visual: Light blue theme visible, Hero shows new headline, Footer shows DSCR link
checkpoints.integration: Lead data reaches CRM (Big Purple Dot)

stories.title: Update color palette to light blue theme
stories.description: As a developer, I want to update globals.css with the new light blue color palette so that the site has a clean, professional look distinct from the DSCR site.
acceptance_criteria: Given globals.css, when colors are updated, then --background is #ffffff, --primary is #2563eb, --primary-dark is #1d4ed8
Given the new palette, when applied, then --surface is #f8fafc, --text-primary is #1e293b, --text-secondary is #64748b
Given the @theme inline block, when updated, then all Tailwind color references use new blue palette
Given amber-specific animations, when reviewed, then convert to blue or remove if not needed
verification_steps: Run npm run dev and inspect CSS variables in browser DevTools
Verify no amber/slate-deep colors remain in :root
stories.notes: Foundation story - must complete before other visual changes. Keep animations like fadeInUp but remove amber-specific ones like pulse-glow.

stories.title: Simplify page.tsx to Hero, Quiz, Footer only
stories.description: As a developer, I want to remove all excess sections from page.tsx so that the site is a minimal single-page lead funnel.
acceptance_criteria: Given page.tsx, when simplified, then only Hero, QualifierQuiz, and Footer components are imported and rendered
Given removed components, when page loads, then TrustBar, HardMoneyRescue, WhatIsHardMoney, RescueOptions, WhyChooseUs, MeetTheTeam, FAQ are not rendered
Given the FAQ schema, when reviewed, then remove the JSON-LD script since FAQ section is removed
verification_steps: Run npm run dev and verify only 3 sections appear
Check page source - no FAQ schema in JSON-LD
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1 for colors. Keep component files for now - will archive in Story 9.

stories.title: Redesign Hero with light theme and new content
stories.description: As a user, I want to see a clean, light-themed hero section so that I understand the service and can easily start the quiz.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Hero, when loaded, then background is clean static light blue gradient (no animated orbs)
Given the headline, when displayed, then it reads 'Need to Exit Your Hard Money Loan?'
Given the subheadline, when displayed, then it reads 'Our dedicated team is here to help you find a solution'
Given the CTA, when displayed, then there is a single 'Get Started' button with blue styling
Given the CTA button, when clicked, then it smooth-scrolls to the quiz section
Given the Hero, when loaded, then urgency badges, value props, phone buttons, and trust indicators are removed
verification_steps: Load page and verify headline text matches spec
Click 'Get Started' and confirm smooth scroll to quiz
Verify no phone numbers or urgency badges appear
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Remove lucide-react icons that are no longer used (Phone, AlertTriangle, Clock, Shield). Keep ArrowRight for button.

stories.title: Update Quiz to light blue theme
stories.description: As a user, I want the quiz to have a light blue theme so that it matches the rest of the redesigned site.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Quiz section, when loaded, then background uses light blue gradient instead of dark slate
Given quiz option cards, when displayed, then they use blue accent colors instead of amber
Given the progress bar, when displayed, then it uses blue gradient instead of amber
Given selected options, when clicked, then highlight uses blue instead of amber
Given the submit button, when displayed, then it uses blue styling
Given focus states, when inputs focused, then outline is blue (#2563eb)
verification_steps: Complete full quiz flow and verify all elements use blue theme
Check that no amber/gold colors appear in quiz
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Keep all quiz logic and validation intact - only change colors.

stories.title: Simplify Quiz success page
stories.description: As a user, I want to see a simple confirmation after submitting the quiz so that I know my request was received.
acceptance_criteria: Given quiz submission success, when displayed, then show 'Thank You, {name}!' heading
Given the success message, when displayed, then show 'We'll be in touch soon.' as the only message
Given the success page, when displayed, then remove 'What happens next' steps section
Given the success page, when displayed, then remove 'Schedule a Call Now' calendar link
Given the success page, when displayed, then remove Call Tanner/Call Zac phone buttons
Given the success page, when displayed, then remove 'Urgent Deal Priority' banner
verification_steps: Complete quiz with test data and verify simple success message
Confirm no phone numbers, calendar links, or action buttons appear
stories.notes: Depends on Story 4. Remove calendars array and getNextCalendar function. Remove Calendar, Clock icons if unused.

stories.title: Simplify Footer
stories.description: As a user, I want a minimal footer so that I can access legal pages and learn about DSCR loans.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Footer, when loaded, then show DSCR cross-sell link to savemydscrloan.com
Given the Footer, when loaded, then remove DPA (arizonadpa.com) cross-sell link
Given the Footer, when loaded, then show Privacy Policy and Disclosures links
Given the Footer, when loaded, then remove all Cornerstone branding and NMLS numbers
Given the Footer, when loaded, then remove Tanner and Zac contact sections
Given the Footer, when loaded, then remove Equal Housing Opportunity badge
Given the Footer, when loaded, then remove legal disclaimer paragraph
Given the Footer, when loaded, then use light blue theme styling
verification_steps: Scroll to footer and verify only DSCR link + legal links appear
Confirm no phone numbers, emails, or NMLS info present
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Remove Phone, Mail icons. Significantly reduces footer size.

stories.title: Update Privacy page to light blue theme
stories.description: As a user, I want the Privacy Policy page to match the main site theme so that the experience is consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Privacy page, when loaded, then background and text colors use light blue palette
Given any links on the page, when displayed, then they use blue (#2563eb) color
verification_steps: Navigate to /privacy and verify light theme is applied
Check that text is readable on light background
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1. May need to update layout.tsx if theme is set there.

stories.title: Update Disclosures page to light blue theme
stories.description: As a user, I want the Disclosures page to match the main site theme so that the experience is consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Disclosures page, when loaded, then background and text colors use light blue palette
Given any links on the page, when displayed, then they use blue (#2563eb) color
verification_steps: Navigate to /disclosures and verify light theme is applied
Check that text is readable on light background
stories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Similar changes to Story 7.

stories.title: Remove unused component files
stories.description: As a developer, I want to delete unused component files so that the codebase is clean and maintainable.
acceptance_criteria: Given unused components, when deleted, then HardMoneyRescue.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then WhatIsHardMoney.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then RescueOptions.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then WhyChooseUs.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then MeetTheTeam.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then FAQ.tsx is removed
Given unused components, when deleted, then TrustBar.tsx is removed
Given the build, when run, then npm run build succeeds with no import errors
verification_steps: Run npm run build and verify no errors
Check components folder only has Hero, QualifierQuiz, Footer, Analytics, ui/
stories.notes: Depends on Story 2. Must ensure page.tsx no longer imports these before deleting.

stories.title: Test locally and verify build
stories.description: As a developer, I want to test the complete site locally so that I can confirm everything works before deployment.
acceptance_criteria: Given npm run dev, when started, then site loads at http://localhost:3000 without errors
Given the quiz flow, when completed, then form submits successfully to /api/submit-lead
Given npm run build, when run, then build completes with no errors
Given all pages, when visited, then /privacy and /disclosures load correctly
Given browser console, when checked, then no JavaScript errors appear
verification_steps: Run npm run dev and complete full quiz flow
Run npm run build and verify success
Check browser console for errors
stories.notes: Depends on Stories 1-9. This is the verification gate before deployment.

stories.title: Deploy to Vercel
stories.description: As a developer, I want to deploy the site to Vercel so that it's accessible on the internet.
acceptance_criteria: Given the codebase, when deployed, then Vercel deployment succeeds
Given the deployment, when complete, then a preview URL is provided
Given the preview URL, when visited, then site loads correctly with all changes
verification_steps: Use /vercel-deploy skill to deploy
Visit preview URL and verify site works
Complete quiz flow on preview URL
stories.notes: Depends on Story 10. Will return preview URL and deployment claim link.

stories.title: Configure Bluehost DNS for domain
stories.description: As a site owner, I want savemyhardmoneyloan.com to point to the Vercel deployment so that users can access the site via the custom domain.
acceptance_criteria: Given Bluehost DNS, when configured, then A record or CNAME points to Vercel
Given Vercel, when domain added, then savemyhardmoneyloan.com is verified
Given the domain, when visited, then site loads with SSL certificate
verification_steps: Add domain in Vercel dashboard
Configure DNS records in Bluehost
Visit https://savemyhardmoneyloan.com and verify site loads
stories.notes: A records updated in Bluehost (@ and www → 76.76.21.21). Vercel alias configured. Site live with SSL at https://savemyhardmoneyloan.com

custom_checks: Site loads at savemyhardmoneyloan.com with SSL

context_notes: This is a hard money rescue lead funnel site. Hard money lending does not require NMLS licensure, so all Cornerstone branding and compliance info is removed. Site acts as a feeder to the DSCR flagship site (savemydscrloan.com). Design should be clean, professional, and fast-loading - utility-focused rather than premium. All leads go through quiz only - no direct phone contact options.
